title: Cannot run container as systemd service
body:
/kind bug

I cannot get any container or pod to run as systemd service and I don't know why. I am aware of #12778, but my problem is not about rootless per se (I think).

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

For clean-up of previous runs, run this:

```bash
sudo systemctl stop bt-container-my-test.service
sleep 2
sudo systemctl disable bt-container-my-test.service
sudo rm -v /etc/systemd/system/bt-container-*.service
rm -v ~/bt-container-*.service
podman kill my-test
podman rm my-test
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo journalctl --rotate
sleep 2
sudo journalctl --vacuum-time=1s
rm -v ~/my-test-status.txt
rm -v ~/my-test-journal.txt
```

Run a container and test it with curl:
```bash
sudo loginctl enable-linger myusername

podman run \
--name my-test \
-p 8080:8080 \
-d \
python bash -c "python3 -m http.server 8080"

sleep 2
curl localhost:8080 -v
podman logs my-test
```

Start the service and check the status:
```bash
cd ~
podman generate systemd --pod-prefix=bt-pod --container-prefix=bt-container --files --name my-test
sudo cp ~/bt-container-my-test.service /etc/systemd/system/
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl start bt-container-my-test.service
sudo systemctl status bt-container-my-test.service | tee ~/my-test-status.txt
sudo journalctl -xe --unit=bt-container-my-test.service | tee ~/my-test-journal.txt
```

**Describe the results you received:**

I get the error message
```
Job for bt-container-my-test.service failed because the control process exited with error code.
See "systemctl status bt-container-my-test.service" and "journalctl -xe" for details.
```
with `my-test-status.txt` containing:
```
● bt-container-my-test.service - Podman bt-container-my-test.service
     Loaded: loaded (/etc/systemd/system/bt-container-my-test.service; disabled; vendor preset: enabled)
     Active: activating (auto-restart) (Result: exit-code) since Fri 2022-01-28 09:20:10 UTC; 15ms ago
       Docs: man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
    Process: 29016 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman start my-test (code=exited, status=125)
    Process: 29062 ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 my-test (code=exited, status=125)
```
and `my-test-journal.txt` containing:
```
-- Logs begin at Fri 2022-01-28 09:19:46 UTC, end at Fri 2022-01-28 09:20:10 UTC. --
Jan 28 09:20:10 croupier-worker systemd[1]: Starting Podman bt-container-my-test.service...
-- Subject: A start job for unit bt-container-my-test.service has begun execution
-- Defined-By: systemd
-- Support: http://www.ubuntu.com/support
-- 
-- A start job for unit bt-container-my-test.service has begun execution.
-- 
-- The job identifier is 7070.
Jan 28 09:20:10 croupier-worker podman[29016]: Error: no container with name or ID "my-test" found: no such container
Jan 28 09:20:10 croupier-worker systemd[1]: bt-container-my-test.service: Control process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
-- Subject: Unit process exited
-- Defined-By: systemd
-- Support: http://www.ubuntu.com/support
-- 
-- An ExecStart= process belonging to unit bt-container-my-test.service has exited.
-- 
-- The process' exit code is 'exited' and its exit status is 125.
Jan 28 09:20:10 croupier-worker podman[29062]: Error: no container with name or ID "my-test" found: no such container
Jan 28 09:20:10 croupier-worker systemd[1]: bt-container-my-test.service: Control process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
-- Subject: Unit process exited
-- Defined-By: systemd
-- Support: http://www.ubuntu.com/support
-- 
-- An ExecStopPost= process belonging to unit bt-container-my-test.service has exited.
-- 
-- The process' exit code is 'exited' and its exit status is 125.
Jan 28 09:20:10 croupier-worker systemd[1]: bt-container-my-test.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
-- Subject: Unit failed
-- Defined-By: systemd
-- Support: http://www.ubuntu.com/support
-- 
-- The unit bt-container-my-test.service has entered the 'failed' state with result 'exit-code'.
Jan 28 09:20:10 croupier-worker systemd[1]: Failed to start Podman bt-container-my-test.service.
-- Subject: A start job for unit bt-container-my-test.service has failed
-- Defined-By: systemd
-- Support: http://www.ubuntu.com/support
-- 
-- A start job for unit bt-container-my-test.service has finished with a failure.
-- 
-- The job identifier is 7070 and the job result is failed.
```
Please note the ` Error: no container with name or ID "my-test" found: no such container`.

**Describe the results you expected:**

A running service with a running container.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2
API Version:  3.4.2
Go Version:   go1.16.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

**Package info (output of  `apt list podman`):**

```
Listing... Done
podman/unknown,now 100:3.4.2-1 amd64 [installed]
podman/unknown 100:3.4.2-1 arm64
podman/unknown 100:3.4.2-1 armhf
podman/unknown 100:3.4.2-1 s390x
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

The host system runs on a virtual server in an on-premise data center.

comments.body:
I have a similar issue. I found removing "--replace" from the  "ExecStart=" line in the .service file helped me fix the problem and now auto starts after enabling it with `systemctl enable container-name`
I am trying to start one container with pihole on raspberry pi 11 arm64.

In my case, the problem was that I used `podman run` instead of `sudo podman run` to start the container. I guess systemd is using root for starting the containers and obviously the container list is user-specific. So there was indeed not container named `my-test` in the root user's container table.

I will try to get my other containers to run. However, I have a feeling as if I will hit the same roadblock as described #12778 in now. We'll see...

When you use rootless podman you have to use `systemctl --user` as described in [podman-generate-systemd(1)](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-generate-systemd.1.md)

@Luap99 The docs you linked only talk about starting a service manually when logged in. Ultimately, I want to start a service during machine startup.  I only tried to start small and get the service running manually first. Sorry for not stating that clearly enough.

Like I wrote above, it works now, but for further endeavors I guess, I will have to look into the workarounds in #12778.
You need to run `loginctl enable-linger <user>` to start your user session at boot and keep it alive after logout.
